description: Shared 9-to-5 working hours between every pair of 223 major world cities, computed for January and July 2026 using IANA time-zone rules. Cities span 42 distinct time-zone regimes.

generated.overlap_definition: Shared minutes of the two local workdays in absolute time, using the shorter of the two ways around the 24-hour clock (dateline-wrapped). Range 0-480.

citation: ClockJumper Research (2026). ClockJumper Global Workday Overlap Dataset, 2026 edition. https://clockjumper.com/research/overlap/dataset. Licensed CC BY 4.0.
city_selection_gates: GaWC 2024 World Cities classification, Alpha/Beta/Gamma tiers (190 cities)
Principal services-delivery hubs per Kearney GSLI top-tier countries and national industry associations (12 cities)
Timezone-regime completeness: the most populous city of any inhabited UTC-offset regime (>=100,000 residents) not otherwise represented (15 cities)
Euromonitor Top 100 City Destinations Index 2025, publicly-released rankings only (6 cities)
weighting_note: Headline statistics can be computed city-weighted (each of 24,753 pairs counts once) or regime-weighted (each of the C(42,2)=861 distinct different-regime pairs counts once). Both are legitimate and answer different questions; see the homepage for the dual-frame explanation.

conventions.no_commas: No field value contains a comma.
conventions.san_jose: 'San Jose (CA)' = California; 'San José (Costa Rica)' = the Costa Rican capital.
